steps.title: Express the given condition in exponential form
steps.content:
Since $f(x) = c^x$, we have $f(6) = c^6$ and $f(4) = c^4$. The condition $f(6) = 9 \cdot f(4)$ becomes:
$$c^6 = 9 \cdot c^4$$

steps.title: Divide by $c^4$ and solve for $c$
steps.content:
Dividing both sides by $c^4$ (valid since $c > 1$ means $c^4 > 0$):
$$c^2 = 9$$
Taking the square root and using $c > 1$:
$$c = 3$$
